126468.explanation: The PCW wants to involve residents through work visits, consultation hours and participation, but a mandatory referendum for important issues imposes a rigid standard. We choose dialogue and input, not a fixed obligation for referenda.

126471.explanation: PCW supports small-scale secondary activities at farms if they fit with livability, traffic safety, and spatial regulations. The words always/must are too forceful: allowing is possible, but through good conditions, permits, and enforcement to prevent nuisance.

126472.explanation: PCW promotes sustainable energy and local ownership. Financial support is possible, although we do not see that as automatic. We choose facilitation and cooperation, not a strict obligation. Support can be provided through knowledge sharing and conditions to maintain support and affordability.

126473.explanation: As PCW we believe we already have more than enough large-scale distribution centers. However, we consider small-scale distribution still compatible with existing and future business parks.

126477.explanation: PCW wants residents to keep freedom in their lifestyle, provided this does not come at the expense of health and livability. The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves may remain allowed, but with attention to education and measures to reduce nuisance and air pollution.

126483.explanation: PCW wants to build for local housing needs within clear plans and while preserving the village character. Individual construction on agricultural land does not fit; spatial quality and infrastructure must be safeguarded.

126485.explanation: PCW values local connectedness and communication, but wants to carefully handle community funds. Additional support is only possible if it demonstrably contributes to community spirit and fits within sound financial frameworks.

126487.explanation: PCW considers music education valuable for development and creativity. However, we do not want generic subsidies for all children. We opt for customized support and cooperation with schools and associations, so assistance reaches those who really need it.

126488.explanation: PCW sees sport as important for social interaction and health. The municipality can help with prerequisites and cooperation, but structural financial support for clubs is only appropriate if necessary and remains within healthy financial limits.

126490.explanation: PCW finds safety important and appreciates enforcement officers (BOAs), but expansion must fit within sound financial frameworks and be proven effective. We prefer more neighborhood police officers for Waddinxveen.

126491.explanation: PCW chooses visible enforcement and cooperation with residents. Installing more cameras does not fit the village character and raises privacy concerns. We prefer to focus on enforcement officers and neighborhood watch.

126493.explanation: PCW wants to build for local housing needs. Priority for local residents in social housing strengthens ties with Waddinxveen and helps starters and families rooted here.

126495.explanation: PCW wants to build for local housing needs. Due to housing shortages, temporary use of holiday homes as a residential address can help, provided this is done carefully and does not compromise spatial quality.

126496.explanation: PCW wants to preserve the village character and green surroundings. New construction takes place within existing frameworks and plans, not by expanding beyond village boundaries. This keeps Waddinxveen livable and recognizable.

126497.explanation: PCW wants everyone to participate. Socially useful work for benefit recipients strengthens involvement and self-reliance, provided it suits them and takes personal circumstances into account.

126498.explanation: As PCW, we believe vulnerable residents deserve our support. Debt remission helps them stay financially afloat and prevents debts. Stopping it would put extra pressure on those already struggling.

126499.explanation: As PCW, we believe aid should remain targeted at those who really need it. A higher income limit makes policy less targeted and more expensive, putting pressure on resources for the most vulnerable.

126501.explanation: PCW wants good charging infrastructure but opts for flexibility and smart solutions to avoid parking pressure. Designating more fixed charging spots is not always necessary; we seek central fast-charging locations and innovative systems that balance space and accessibility.

126502.explanation: As PCW, we find traffic safety important, especially for children and the elderly. A maximum speed of 30 km/h increases safety and livability in residential areas. Smart street design remains essential. At the same time, some roads within built-up areas are more suitable for 50 km/h.

126503.explanation: As PCW, we believe care should be nearby and appropriate. Freedom of choice for a care provider who aligns with faith, culture, or beliefs is important for trust and quality of care.

126505.explanation: As PCW, we consider solid finances important. Lowering the property tax (OZB) sounds attractive, but must not come at the expense of investments in facilities. Increasing the OZB is seen as a last resort.

126507.explanation: As PCW, we believe taxes should not rise unnecessarily. A higher tourist tax does not fit with the village character of Waddinxveen and may hinder social interaction and hospitality. We choose a fair and stable distribution of burdens.

126508.explanation: As PCW, we believe several large zoning plans for business park expansion have already been approved recently. We carefully monitor whether more is needed. Expansion is done with attention to sustainability and livability.

126510.explanation: As PCW, we believe everyone should have a fair chance at housing. Priority for permit holders increases pressure on social housing and lengthens waiting times for residents. We choose a balanced distribution.

126512.explanation: As PCW, we believe agricultural land should primarily remain for food production. Solar panels belong on roofs and suitable locations, not on fertile land. This protects our agricultural base and landscape.

126514.explanation: As PCW, we find sport important but a new sports park on Parklaan is not necessary. We prefer expansion at sports park 't Suyt for both hockey and the BeFair extension.

126516.explanation: As PCW, we believe a coffeeshop does not fit with the village and safe character of Waddinxveen. Allowing one increases risks of drug use and nuisance. We choose prevention and a healthy living environment.

126517.explanation: As PCW, we find affordable housing important. At least 30 percent social housing in new construction helps starters and people with limited budgets. This builds a balanced and connected community.

126523.explanation: PCW wants to improve traffic safety on Brugweg. Heavy traffic does not belong in residential areas; it should be routed via a new ring road. This gives cyclists and pedestrians more space and maintains neighborhood livability.

126524.explanation: PCW values Sunday quietness as a precious village quality. Allowing more events on Sundays does not fit this principle. We choose small-scale, local activities and encounters, but no expansion of large-scale Sunday events to preserve peace and connectedness.

126468.explanation: The CDA values citizen involvement in important decisions. Good participation upfront is paramount. Referenda can be additional but must not undermine the role of the municipal council and careful decision-making.

126471.explanation: The CDA sees opportunities for secondary activities at farms to keep the countryside vital. That cannot be unlimited: it must fit the environment, zoning, and livability. Tailored approaches and good consideration remain necessary.

126472.explanation: The CDA stimulates local initiatives for sustainable energy. If residents want to generate energy together, the municipality can support, for example with advice or financial contributions, provided it is feasible and contributes to affordable energy for residents.

126473.explanation: The CDA wants to counteract the 'concretization' of Waddinxveen. New distribution centers put pressure on landscape, traffic, and livability and provide relatively little local employment. We choose a varied and future-proof business offering.

126477.explanation: The CDA wants to reduce nuisance and health risks from wood burning but not impose a general ban. We focus on awareness, the Burning Guide (Stookwijzer), and tailored solutions, with attention to health, livability, and responsible use.

126483.explanation: The CDA recognizes that tailor-made solutions for farmers can contribute to vitality and business succession. Housing construction in rural areas is not automatic. It must fit landscape, livability, and existing rules, with an eye on the long term.

126485.explanation: The CDA recognizes the importance of a strong local broadcaster for information, associations, and social cohesion. The municipality can support the local broadcaster to maintain local news and engagement in Waddinxveen.

126487.explanation: The CDA values music education for children’s development. We encourage broad access via schools, clubs, and existing schemes. Subsidies can be targeted to those who need it, without making it a general obligation.

126488.explanation: Sports clubs are the glue of society. If they are struggling, the municipality can provide temporary financial support, for example for maintenance or sustainability, provided there is perspective for a healthy and future-proof club.

126490.explanation: The CDA wants residents to feel safe in their neighborhood and shopping area. Additional enforcement officers increase visibility and prevention and strengthen cooperation with the police and neighborhood teams. This way, we address nuisance and insecurity more quickly and effectively.

126491.explanation: The CDA sees camera surveillance as a complementary tool to enhance safety. It can be used in specific locations where necessary, with clear objectives and respect for privacy. Prevention and the presence of people always remain the most important.

126493.explanation: The CDA understands the desire to offer local housing seekers perspective. At the same time, there are regional agreements and legal rules. We focus on sufficient affordable housing and a fair distribution, keeping an eye on ties to Waddinxveen where possible.

126495.explanation: The CDA believes vacation homes are intended for recreation. Permanent residence often leads to problems with safety, facilities, and livability. We prefer to focus on sufficient regular housing that matches the needs of residents.

126496.explanation: The CDA wants to handle our scarce space carefully. We preferably build within existing village boundaries, considering livability, greenery, and facilities. New construction outside the village is not automatic and requires a very careful assessment.

126497.explanation: The CDA believes in participation according to ability. Socially meaningful work can contribute to structure and self-worth, but coercion is not always appropriate. We choose customization, guidance, and what a person can do, with attention to health and personal circumstances.

126498.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality must have an eye for financially vulnerable people. Exemption from municipal taxes helps prevent problems and debts. We protect these residents and keep the burdens fairly distributed.

126499.explanation: The CDA wants to prevent people from falling through the cracks. By raising the income threshold for poverty assistance, working families with low incomes can also receive support. This way, we offer timely help and prevent bigger problems.

126501.explanation: The CDA wants to enable the transition to electric driving. More charging points, especially in residential areas and near stations, help residents without a private driveway. We do this balanced, considering scarce parking space and livability.

126502.explanation: The CDA opts for traffic safety with customization. 30 km/h is suitable in residential streets and around schools, but a general reduction throughout the entire built-up area is not desirable. Good design and clear roads are more effective than one standard.

126503.explanation: The CDA believes care should be personal and people-centered. Where possible, people should be able to choose a care provider that aligns with their religion, culture, or belief, within legal frameworks and maintaining quality and accessibility of care.

126505.explanation: The CDA wants to keep housing costs for residents as low as possible. If the financial situation permits, property tax should be lowered. We critically examine municipal expenses and efficiency first before increasing burdens on residents.

126507.explanation: The CDA wants Waddinxveen to remain hospitable and accessible. A higher tourist tax can harm local entrepreneurs and small-scale recreation. We keep this tax reasonable and balanced with stimulating the local economy and facilities.

126508.explanation: The CDA recognizes the importance of a strong local business climate. Expansion of business parks is possible, but not unlimited. It must fit accessibility, livability, and landscape, preferably with more intensive and smarter use of existing sites.

126510.explanation: The CDA believes social rental homes must be fairly distributed. Refugees with residence permits deserve good guidance, but structural priority increases pressure on other housing seekers. We focus on customization and sufficient housing for everyone.

126512.explanation: The CDA wants to use agricultural land sparingly. Solar panels preferably belong on roofs and existing buildings. Agricultural land is intended for food production and landscape. Large-scale solar fields on agricultural land do not fit with that.

126514.explanation: The CDA sees the importance of good sports facilities for health and community life. A new sports park can be an option, but only if location, accessibility, livability, and costs are carefully considered and there is sufficient support in the area.

126516.explanation: The CDA is cautious about establishing a coffeeshop. This can lead to nuisance and safety risks. We choose prevention, enforcement, and a safe living environment, and see no need for a coffeeshop in Waddinxveen.

126517.explanation: The CDA wants sufficient affordable housing for people with lower or middle incomes. A minimum share of 30 percent social rental in new construction fits a balanced living mix and ensures that starters, families, and seniors can continue to live in Waddinxveen.

126523.explanation: The CDA wants to improve traffic safety on Brugweg. A general ban on truck traffic is a drastic measure. We choose customization: reducing risks with targeted measures, clear routes, and enforcement, combined with better alternatives.

126524.explanation: The CDA values peace and space for social encounters. Events on Sunday can contribute to liveliness but require careful consideration. We choose customization, respecting neighbors, volunteers, and the character of Sunday.

126468.explanation: The municipal council is elected to make decisions and take responsibility. Involving residents is good, but referenda are not the right instrument for that.

126471.explanation: Space for entrepreneurship is good, but customization remains necessary. Not every location is suitable for extra functions due to traffic, nature, or livability.

126472.explanation: Local energy cooperatives increase support and accelerate sustainability. Support is logical but should come from the national or provincial government.

126473.explanation: Distribution centers create jobs but also use space and cause traffic. Therefore, we believe it's important to locate distribution centers outside residential areas. The VVD's principle is: move first, then build.

126477.explanation: Prohibition goes too far. However, it is logical to take nuisance and air quality seriously and to hold residents accountable for responsible use.

126483.explanation: Additional housing can help with scarcity but must fit spatial planning. No uncontrolled growth, but customization.

126485.explanation: A local broadcaster is important for local news. The subsidy currently granted is sufficient in our view.

126487.explanation: Culture is important but not everything needs to be funded by the municipality. Targeted support is better than a generic scheme.

126488.explanation: Sports clubs are social glue but must meet the needs of residents.

126490.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety and livability.

126491.explanation: Cameras can help tackle crime. Deployment must focus on problem locations.

126493.explanation: Local ties matter. Residents who live or work here should keep prospects for housing, within legal frameworks.

126495.explanation: Permanent residence does not structurally solve the housing shortage and can put pressure on parks.

126496.explanation: To solve housing shortages, building outside existing village boundaries is also necessary.

126497.explanation: Participation is better than standing aside. Socially useful work increases opportunities and self-worth, considering what a person can do.

126498.explanation: Exemption is a safety net. Stopping it without alternatives increases problems. Abuse must be prevented.

126499.explanation: Working poor should not fall through the cracks. Expansion may be needed, provided it is affordable and temporary. Expansion in Waddinxveen is not necessary because we have already raised the income threshold.

126501.explanation: Electric driving is increasing. Additional charging points are needed, but balanced with parking space for everyone.

126502.explanation: Traffic safety is important, and attention must be given to good traffic flow.

126503.explanation: Freedom of choice is important, but the municipality is responsible for ensuring quality. Too many care providers leads to a decrease in quality care.

126505.explanation: Housing costs are under pressure. Lower property tax (ozb) gives residents relief and forces the municipality to remain strict on expenditures.

126507.explanation: Tourism also generates income. An increase is possible, as long as it does not make Waddinxveen less attractive.

126508.explanation: Business activity creates jobs. Expansion is possible, provided it is well integrated and takes traffic and the environment into account.

126510.explanation: Equal rules for everyone. Priority undermines trust and increases waiting times for other home seekers.

126512.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce. Solar panels should primarily be placed on roofs and industrial areas, not on fertile ground. Custom solutions must always be possible.

126514.explanation: Sports facilities are important, but there is no space for them on the Parklaan.

126516.explanation: A coffeeshop does not belong in Waddinxveen. There is more suitable space for this in an urban environment.

126517.explanation: A mix of housing is important, but it should not become impossible to build homes due to overly strict policies. Customization is needed.

126523.explanation: Alternative routes must be available, but there must also be room for entrepreneurs to conduct business.

126524.explanation: Events create liveliness. Sunday rest is fine, but it is no reason for unnecessary restrictions.

126468.explanation: A referendum is no replacement for participation. But it can help if there is a major social impact, the council is divided, or residents clearly feel a need for a voting moment. WEWA wants participation from the very start.

126471.explanation: WEWA has a positive attitude towards diversifying farming businesses. This strengthens the local economy and increases connection between farmers and society. It must fit the landscape and the scale of Waddinxveen.

126472.explanation: The municipality should facilitate rather than hinder, by providing space in policies. Helping with locations, information, and permits, and ensuring projects don’t get stuck in bureaucracy.

126473.explanation: The available space in industrial areas is needed for relocating companies within Waddinxveen.

126477.explanation: WEWA is positive about allowing fireplaces and wood stoves, as long as they are used responsibly, cause no nuisance, and the municipality enforces clear rules and provides information.

126483.explanation: The green boundaries of Waddinxveen are important. Littering, setting precedents, and loss of social quality must be prevented.

126485.explanation: The current subsidy for the RTW is sufficient.

126487.explanation: There is already a scheme whereby young people under 18 do not pay VAT on music lessons.

126488.explanation: Associations are responsible for their own financial basis. The municipality can support with advice and conditions, but not with money.

126490.explanation: WEWA values safety but does not believe simply adding more enforcement officers is the most effective solution. Smart deployment works better than more personnel.

126491.explanation: Camera surveillance is a targeted method to increase safety where needed.

126493.explanation: WEWA wants it to be easier for Waddinxveen residents to continue living here.

126495.explanation: WEWA wants to invest in real housing construction, not in temporary solutions that cause problems later.

126496.explanation: WEWA wants to preserve the green boundaries.

126497.explanation: Support must be customized and aimed at real help.

126498.explanation: The municipality's financial stability must be safeguarded. Targeted support is better than a blank exemption.

126499.explanation: The poverty threshold in Waddinxveen has already been raised to 125 percent.

126501.explanation: The demand for charging points is growing rapidly. Infrastructure must be ready for the future.

126502.explanation: In Waddinxveen, only the main routes are still 50 km/h; in residential areas, the speed limit is already 30 km/h.

126503.explanation: It is important to keep care affordable. A large diversity of providers is inefficient and confusing.

126505.explanation: The municipality must maintain a financially healthy budget. The property tax (ozb) is relatively low in Waddinxveen.

126507.explanation: The tourist tax in Waddinxveen has not been increased for years.

126508.explanation: The green boundaries must be preserved.

126510.explanation: All home seekers must have an equal chance.

126512.explanation: There are better alternatives for placing solar panels, such as on buildings, parking garages, or noise barriers.

126514.explanation: The Parklaan is not suitable for realizing a large sports park. There are better locations elsewhere.

126516.explanation: A regulated coffeeshop is safer than illegal street trade. It is controllable and better protects young people.

126517.explanation: There is a great need for social rental housing. The waiting lists are too long.

126523.explanation: The road is too narrow, too vulnerable, and too residential-oriented.

126524.explanation: Municipal policy must align with how people live today. The era of strict Sunday restrictions is over.

126468.explanation: Residents can express their opinions in many different ways. Engaging in dialogue yields much more than a referendum. The question is also what you would do with the result of a referendum—whether to adopt it anyway, or decide otherwise, as was the case with plastic collection.

126471.explanation: It is not a right to open a shop or campsite on your own property. Sometimes there are reasons not to allow this (for example, because environmental regulations prohibit it). However, we want to explore how to support good initiatives.

126472.explanation: We want to support good initiatives; this does not always have to be financial—sometimes knowledge support is sufficient. If it can be done without financial support from the municipality, that is also fine.

126473.explanation: The location of Waddinxveen is perfect for setting up distribution centers. However, Waddinxveen must remain livable. In our view, expanding distribution centers is not part of that.

126477.explanation: Burning wood in fireplaces and wood stoves produces fine particulate emissions. Fine particles are harmful to the health of humans and animals.

126483.explanation: Requests for this should be assessed against the applicable rules and guidelines.

126485.explanation: The regional broadcaster must have sufficient funding to perform its task. If that is not possible, we are happy to discuss what is needed.

126487.explanation: Music is a permanent subject at primary school. Outside school, children can take music lessons, which parents pay for themselves. If parents are unable to afford this, they can apply for the minimum income scheme.

126488.explanation: Provided that the sports clubs themselves have done everything to maintain financially healthy policies. Sports deliver so much socially that we must encourage it. If a little extra support is needed, we are happy to explore how to make that possible.

126490.explanation: The number of full-time enforcement officers grows proportionally with the number of residents. We see no sufficient reason to deviate from this.

126491.explanation: At this moment, we see insufficient reasons to allow this.

126493.explanation: We find it more important what someone can contribute to the local community than where they currently live. For example, we prefer to prioritize a teacher or someone working in healthcare for Waddinxveen over someone who already lives in Waddinxveen.

126495.explanation: When a home meets all requirements according to the Building Code, such a request can be granted. More flexible rules apply to holiday homes. However, this does not seem to be relevant in Waddinxveen.

126496.explanation: D66 believes that no construction should take place outside the current boundaries (although development of existing designated industrial sites is acceptable).

126497.explanation: Receiving social assistance is not a luxury. Nobody wants to rely on it. If you are ill and therefore unable to work and receive social assistance, D66 will not ask you to do socially useful work. We are happy to help those who want and can leave social assistance.

126498.explanation: If someone cannot pay municipal taxes, the municipality should forgive them. Income is not the only criterion for this; assets also matter.

126499.explanation: The minimum income policy currently uses 130 percent. This is good so that you do not face a poverty trap as soon as you rise just above the absolute minimum.

126501.explanation: We see that charging points are well used. Electric driving should be given more advantages compared to fossil fuel driving.

126502.explanation: A smaller speed difference reduces traffic risks.

126503.explanation: Unfortunately, this is not always possible due to a shortage of healthcare providers. People who need care receive a caregiver who matches their experience, culture, or beliefs.

126505.explanation: The property tax (ozb) does not need to be higher than necessary, but it does not have to be lowered by definition. There are sufficient ambitions that cannot yet be fulfilled due to financial choices, so there is no reason to reduce the property tax.

126507.explanation: Waddinxveen offers little opportunity for tourist tax, so increasing this (beyond inflation correction) makes no sense.

126508.explanation: There is still sufficient space in the industrial areas. When these seem to become full, we will review whether expansion is necessary.

126510.explanation: These people are waiting for a fresh start in their lives. We believe it is good to provide that. This creates room to alleviate the asylum chain and possibly reduce the number of shelters in the long term.

126512.explanation: Entrepreneurs are free to make their own choice in this. We see no reason for a municipal ban.

126514.explanation: A new building will be constructed, including a sports hall with two gym halls, a canteen, child care, etc.

126516.explanation: This is not an issue at present.

126517.explanation: We support the current housing care vision, which states 30 percent social rental housing. However, this cannot be realized in every separate project; therefore, the 30 percent applies across all new construction projects. We do want sufficient distribution across neighborhoods.

126523.explanation: We do not want through freight traffic on Brugweg. We make an exception for destination traffic, such as garbage trucks and moving vans.

126524.explanation: Everyone may spend Sunday as they see fit. Allowing more events on Sundays could contribute to this.

126468.explanation: A referendum provides insight into what residents want. Participation and input from residents from the start is a given. It must be clear what residents want. If the council or the Executive deviates from the position of the involved residents, they must explain why.

126471.explanation: If the limited number of farmers want to earn income in other ways, that is possible within agreed limits. Whether that is always the case can be assessed on a case-by-case basis.

126472.explanation: Within the energy transition, appropriate possibilities must be supported, even if this leads to unforeseen solutions.

126473.explanation: There is little land available for expansion of distribution centers. We must therefore handle this very cautiously.

126477.explanation: Bans are very difficult, but we believe stricter rules can be introduced for wood burning, for example by restricting burning during calm wind conditions.

126483.explanation: To a limited extent, agreements can be made about this within pre-established standards.

126485.explanation: The local broadcaster is well listened to and fulfills a clear function within the community.

126487.explanation: Initially, it is the schools' own responsibility, but external support from the 'Culture with Quality' project must be minimally supported by the municipality. Moreover, music is important for every child's development. Culture is for everyone.

126488.explanation: It is also in the municipality's interest to keep the community lively and vital.

126490.explanation: More enforcement does not seem necessary at this time. However, safety must remain a priority.

126491.explanation: Camera surveillance does not necessarily increase safety, but it can be applied selectively where there is demonstrable concern about the feeling of safety.

126493.explanation: Houses should remain available not only for current Waddinxveen residents but also for new inhabitants.

126495.explanation: As part of the housing problem and in urgent cases, this can contribute to solving the housing shortage.

126496.explanation: In a fully built-up environment, it remains important to have green buffers that substantially contribute to living comfort, nature, and recreation.

126497.explanation: Recipients of social assistance can be encouraged to contribute, but they cannot be forced.

126498.explanation: Forgiving debts can help residents in debt to make a fresh start.

126499.explanation: The minimum income policy will be expanded to include low incomes just outside the rules, so they do not suffer a net loss.

126501.explanation: Waddinxveen is certainly not lagging behind, but a better balance is necessary for the future—with more electric cars.

126502.explanation: Residential neighborhoods will be standardly designed as 30 km/h zones, with layouts that actively encourage slower driving.

126503.explanation: Where possible, this is also desirable, but it does not have to be automatic.

126505.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is a source of municipal income. At the same time, it should not become a cash cow. Regional context is considered when determining its rate.

126507.explanation: Tourist tax can be adjusted to inflation levels. An increase is not necessarily required.

126508.explanation: We must handle the remaining space carefully. We believe there are limits to building within the built-up area.

126510.explanation: This is determined by legislation. The idea that the housing problem is caused by status holders is incorrect.

126512.explanation: There are conceivable situations where the installation of (small) solar fields can be made possible within the applicable agreements.

126514.explanation: If space still allows, an additional sports facility in Parkwijk Triangel could be an option to offer local residents more opportunities to exercise nearby.

126516.explanation: As far as it can solve a problem, it is good to determine whether it (partly) addresses the issue.

126517.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA has already called attention to building more affordable owner-occupied homes in the Triangel. The goal: more affordable housing to meet at least the Housing and Care vision of 30 percent social housing per project.

126523.explanation: This should be expressed more clearly in the interest of residents. However, it requires a broader regional approach to tackle the current issues effectively and to properly assess the consequences.

126524.explanation: With respect for differing opinions, GroenLinks-PvdA believes many more activities should be possible on Sundays. They have regularly advocated for this. As a result, rules have been adjusted to allow more events on Sundays.

statements.title: The municipality must allow more events on Sundays.

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
In Waddinxveen the business parks may expand.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Waddinxveen must deploy more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Waddinxveen must have increased camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Waddinxveen must build a new sports park on Parklaan.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: At least 30% of the newly built homes in Waddinxveen must be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Waddinxveen moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: In Waddinxveen the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves must remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Waddinxveen moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The residents of Waddinxveen must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Waddinxveen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality must ban truck traffic on the Brugweg.
